# SPEC — podcast-show-notes

**Generated by claude-skill-architect v0.2**
**Date:** 2026-04-27

## What you're trying to achieve

> I run a weekly podcast on AI tooling. After every recording, writing the show notes for the publishing platform takes me 90 minutes — pulling out the timestamps, summarising segments, listing the tools and links mentioned, drafting an episode summary that sounds like me. I want a skill that takes a transcript file and produces show notes I can publish without editing. Voice should match my prior episodes.

## Role and voice

- **Role:** "You are a pragmatic podcast-notes engineer producing publishable show notes from raw transcripts."
- **Voice characteristics:**
  - Pragmatic, not effusive
  - Trade-offs explicit (e.g. when a section is thin, say so)
  - Matches the host's voice profile (distilled from prior episodes)
  - No hedging, no padding, no sycophancy

## When it fires

- **Trigger pattern:** Command-triggered — `/show-notes <transcript-path>` or pasted transcript
- **Consumer:** the host (operator); output published unedited to the podcast platform
- **Frequency:** weekly per podcast

## Output

- **Shape:** Structured document (markdown)
- **Ship-as-is:** "If I read it once and would publish it without editing."
- **Needs-edit:** Same content but requires light voice tuning, a missing timestamp, or a misattributed tool link.
- **Rewrite threshold:** Sounds AI-generic; sections padded; voice profile drift visible from the first paragraph.

## Optimise targets

- Voice fidelity to the host's prior 10 episodes
- Speed: zero edits before publish on a clean transcript
- Structural reliability: every required section present, every link accurate
- Honest scope: when the transcript is too short for a section, the skill says so

## Anti-optimise (DO / DON'T pairs)

**General:**
- DON'T pad thin sections — DO mark them and let the host decide whether to cut or expand
- DON'T invent tools or links that weren't in the transcript — DO list only what was actually mentioned
- DON'T flatten the host's voice into generic podcast-notes voice — DO match the distilled voice profile

**AI-slop defaults to refuse** *(from Probe G):*
- DON'T use generic templates / "standard advice" — DO write specific to this episode
- DON'T hedge ("you might find...", "it could be worth...") — DO state directly
- DON'T produce comprehensive 800-word summaries when a tight 150-word one is sharper — DO match the distilled length norms
- DON'T add sycophantic intros ("In this fascinating episode...") — DO open with the actual hook from the conversation

## Response contract

**Always include in every output:**
- Episode title and number
- One-paragraph summary in the host's voice
- Section breakdown with timestamps
- Tools / links mentioned

**Include when relevant:**
- Guest bio — when a guest appeared
- Quote pulls — when a memorable line surfaces

## Reasoning discipline

`N/A — outputs are descriptive, not recommendation-driven`

## Mode inference

`N/A — single mode`

## Architecture

- **Structure:** Multi-component. SKILL.md orchestrates; references hold the voice profile and the section template; one helper script for transcript chunking.
- **Progressive disclosure layers:**
  - **Level 1 — Metadata** (`name` + `description`).
  - **Level 2 — SKILL.md body.** Pipeline: parse transcript → chunk if needed → match voice → fill section template → validate against ship-as-is bar → emit.
  - **Level 3 — References and scripts.** Voice profile, section template, chunker script.
- **Modes:** Single.
- **References:**
  - `references/voice-profile.md` — distilled from the host's prior 10 episodes; loaded on every invocation
  - `references/section-template.md` — the canonical structure of show notes; loaded on every invocation
- **Scripts/utilities:**
  - `scripts/chunk-transcript.py` — splits long transcripts into sub-chunks at natural breakpoints; called when transcript > N tokens; I/O: file path in → list of (start_ts, end_ts, text) chunks out

## Build scope (for the agent building this skill)

- **Research:** Read the host's last 10 published show notes (path provided by user via config). Distil voice profile (sentence length norms, vocabulary, opening patterns, hedging avoidance).
- **Knowledge codification:** Produce `references/voice-profile.md` and `references/section-template.md`. Voice profile schema: opening pattern; sentence-length distribution; characteristic phrases; phrases to avoid; section length norms.
- **Tools/utilities:** Build `scripts/chunk-transcript.py` per the I/O contract above. Validate on a known long transcript before declaring complete.
- **Validation:** (a) Fresh-Claude trigger test — paste a transcript, confirm `/show-notes` triggers the skill correctly. (b) Run on three real prior episodes; confirm output matches what was published, ±light edits. (c) Run on a deliberately too-short transcript; confirm the skill pushes back rather than padding.
- **Setup/config:** `config.json` with `show_notes_archive_path` (string, required) and `voice_profile_refresh_episodes` (integer, default 10).
- **Usability:**
  - **Discovery:** the slash command `/show-notes` and natural-language triggers ("draft show notes for this episode") MUST both work.
  - **First-time setup:** if `config.json` is missing or `show_notes_archive_path` is not set, the skill MUST prompt the user before running (one question, MC: "where's your archive?").
  - **Daily invocation:** typical use is paste-and-run; the skill SHOULD complete in under 60 seconds for a 60-minute transcript.
  - **Output interpretation:** output is a single markdown file; no interpretation required beyond paste-to-publish.

## v1 scope

- Single mode, command-triggered.
- Reads transcript from path or paste.
- Produces show notes following the canonical section template, matched to the voice profile.
- Includes the **non-content section detection** (10x proposal #3 baked in): the skill auto-detects and excludes intros, ads, and sign-offs from content chunking.
- Pushes back on too-short transcripts rather than padding.

## 10x potential (parked)

- **Periodic voice-profile re-distillation** — re-distil from the most recent N episodes so the voice profile tracks evolution. Rationale: prevents voice drift over time as the host's style changes. Park: needs a stable v1 first.
- **JSON sidecar emission** — same content as a structured JSON file for direct podcast-platform API upload. Rationale: skips the manual paste step. Park: useful once a few episodes have been through v1.

## Declined enhancements

- **Voice-drift sentence flagging** — flag sentences in the draft that drift more than X% from voice profile. User reason: "adds another quality bar I'd have to tune; the voice profile + my read should be enough for v1."

## Pushback triggers

- When the transcript is too short to fill the canonical section structure, the skill MUST push back: "transcript is short for [section]; cut the section or extend the recording — don't pad."
- When the user requests show notes from a transcript with less than [N] words of substantive content, the skill MUST refuse and ask the user to confirm.

## Known failure modes

- **Voice drift in the draft.** Mitigation: voice profile loaded on every invocation; build agent's validation tests one prior episode for fidelity check.
- **Invented tools or links.** Mitigation: extraction is grounded — tools/links MUST be quoted from the transcript with timestamps.
- **Padding to fill sections.** Mitigation: the skill explicitly pushes back rather than padding (see Pushback triggers).
- **Generic AI-podcast-notes voice.** Mitigation: voice profile + Probe G defaults explicitly refused in anti-optimise.

## Operational disciplines for shipping

- Test in a fresh Claude instance — paste a transcript and confirm trigger + output shape without dev-conversation context bleed.
- Build the evaluation viewer before writing test cases — render side-by-side: skill output vs published version of three prior episodes.
- Externalise config to `config.json` — `show_notes_archive_path` and `voice_profile_refresh_episodes`.

## Version

v0.1 — 2026-04-27
